title: Report: Mass. drivers have paid more than $1 billion extra at the pump since the Iran war began
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts drivers have paid over $1 billion extra at the pump since the Iran conflict began in February.
Gas prices nationwide rose from under $2 to over $4 per gallon, costing Americans an additional $8.4 billion.
Home heating costs in Massachusetts increased 37.9% since 2025, with oil costs up $224 per tank.

title: Mass. campgrounds still have availability for World Cup fans
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Canoe River Campground in Mansfield has availability for World Cup fans; rates range from $51 to $187 per night.
Jellystone Park Cranberry Acres in Carver offers glamping cabins starting at $300 per night, available during World Cup dates.
Boston hotels are raising prices during World Cup games; some are offering luxury packages, such as a $75K ultra-luxe option.

title: This stroke survivor is running Boston for Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Tim Rafferty, a stroke survivor, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital.
Rafferty suffered a hemorrhagic stroke in 2019 that left him paralyzed on his left side but recovered with Spaulding's help.
After successfully completing the Boston Marathon in 2021, Rafferty aims to run again to give back to Spaulding Hospital.

title: ‘I appreciated that’: Jayson Tatum credited Knicks fans’ applause in MSG return
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to Madison Square Garden for the first time since his injury last May, playing 40 minutes in a Celtics loss.
Tatum was apprehensive about playing at MSG, but appreciated Knicks fans' applause during introductions despite team rivalry.
Celtics lost 112-106 to the Knicks; Boston's next game is against the Pelicans on April 10 at 7:30 p.m.

title: Everett woman arrested after dog attack injures pet and owner
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 49-year-old Everett woman was arrested on outstanding warrants after her dog attacked another dog and its owner.
The dog, an 11-month-old hound, is quarantined; the attacked dog, Mushroom, needed extensive medical treatment.
The woman was arrested Thursday night and will be arraigned in Malden District Court, according to Everett police.

title: Wife of Maine father who died saving his children opens up about their ‘love story’
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Ryan Jennings, 46, drowned saving two of his children from a riptide at Juno Beach, Florida, on April 1.
Jennings' wife, Emily, shared their love story and his heroic legacy in a tribute post on social media.
Two GoFundMe pages have raised over $242,000 for the Jennings family after his tragic death.

title: A Vermont ski lift is being retired after six decades
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Pico Mountain's Outpost Double chairlift, installed in 1969, is being retired due to a main drive shaft failure.
A full replacement for the retired lift won't be available next season due to permitting requirements.
Pico Mountain plans to auction the Outpost chairs this summer as they explore temporary terrain servicing options.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com offers a news quiz to test knowledge on Greater Boston's latest trending stories.
The quiz is crafted by Peter Chianca, Boston.com's general assignment editor since 2019.
Peter Chianca has a background in news editing, column writing, and music reporting in Greater Boston.

title: Jayson Tatum returns to MSG, but Knicks hold off Celtics late: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to Madison Square Garden for the first time since his Achilles injury, scoring 24 points against the Knicks.
The Knicks defeated the Boston Celtics 112-106, maintaining the possibility of securing the 2-seed in the playoffs.
Baylor Scheierman and Payton Pritchard provided key scoring for the Celtics with 20 and 23 points, respectively, in the game.

title: His brother passed away from glioblastoma. He’s running Boston for cancer research
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Colin Gerner is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in memory of his brother who died of glioblastoma.
Gerner co-founded StacheStrong, raising millions for brain cancer research and awarding a $1 million grant to Mass General.
Gerner aims to raise over $200,000 in this marathon, funding the first year of crucial glioblastoma research in Boston.
